title: Use Go To To Drop Off An AGV At The Hospital During A Quick Recovery Scenario
summary: Use the Robot Selection Go-to function as a quick recovery aid in the example scenario where a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave. The source states the operator can remove and add the AGV, then use Go to to send it to a selected square at the hospital, and that the AGV will pick up a new task once its tote has been removed.
when_to_use: Use in the source-described quick recovery example when equipment is down, specifically when a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave, and the AGV is being repositioned to the hospital using the Go-to function.
not_for: Do not use as a fully operationalized remove/add AGV procedure; the source states to remove and add the AGV but does not define how to perform that subprocedure.
Do not use when a different recovery path is required; this source only supports the specific example recovery scenario described.

Do not assume this source authorizes AGV removal on an active system without role or site approval; related packet evidence notes access may be restricted and warns against removing an AGV on an active system.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the AGV, use the Go-to function, choose the destination square, confirm the move, and remove the tote as described in the source.
May be needed if remove/add AGV permissions are restricted, based on packet evidence indicating not all users have credentials and supervisors may have access.

safety_notes: The source frames this as a quick recovery example, not a complete controlled maintenance procedure.
The source does not define the remove and add AGV subprocedure; obtain SME review before operationalizing that portion.
Packet evidence indicates removing an AGV on an active system may be a restricted or high-risk action.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the OptiSweep map screen
Ability to remove the tote from the AGV

access_required: Access to the map or Map Monitor view where AGVs can be selected
Permission to use the Go-to function
Permission to perform remove/add AGV actions if required by site controls
system_preconditions: The scenario matches the source example: a tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave.
The AGV can be identified on the map.
The AGV is eligible for Go-to use as described by the source context for AGVs not in a task.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the Robot Selection Go-to function, the quick recovery example, and the visible steps: select an AGV, select Go to, select a square, and confirm.

steps.title: Identify the affected AGV on the map
steps.purpose: Locate the AGV involved in the quick recovery scenario before issuing a Go-to command.
steps.instruction: On the map, identify the AGV involved in the recovery scenario where a tipper goes down and the AGV will not leave.
steps.expected_result: The affected AGV is visually identified on the map and ready for selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct AGV can be distinguished and selected on the map.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be identified on the map or the operator cannot determine which AGV is affected.

actions.label: Locate AGV on map
actions.exact_action: Identify the affected AGV on the map in the described recovery scenario where equipment is down and the AGV will not leave.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection Go-to slide and map-selection context showing that the AGV is selected from the map.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the affected AGV cannot be confidently identified on the map.
Stop and escalate if the map view does not allow AGV selection.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: This is helpful when trying to recover quickly. Example if a Tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave...
Nearby transcript says these are objects that can be selected on the map and that clicking the map alone does nothing because the map needs to know whether the user is selecting an AGV or a cell.

steps.title: Remove and add the AGV
steps.purpose: Perform the source-stated recovery preparation before using Go to.
steps.instruction: Remove and add the AGV as stated in the source for this recovery example. Because the source does not define how to perform this subprocedure, use only approved site-specific controls and permissions.
steps.expected_result: The AGV has been removed and added back as part of the example recovery setup.
steps.healthy_condition: The remove/add action is completed using approved access and the AGV remains available for the next Go-to action.
steps.failure_condition: The operator does not have permission, the remove/add action cannot be completed, or the AGV state does not allow continuation.
stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the operator does not have credentials to remove and add the AGV.
Stop and escalate if site policy does not permit AGV removal on an active system.
Stop and escalate because the source does not provide the remove/add subprocedure details.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can remove and add the agv and use the go to function to drop it off at the hospital.

Nearby transcript warns that on an active system one of the worst actions is removing an AGV, notes that not all users have credentials, and says operators should not have access while supervisors may.

steps.title: Select the AGV
steps.purpose: Choose the AGV as the object for the Go-to action.
steps.instruction: Select the AGV on the map.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is selected as the active object in the interface.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV selection is accepted and the interface is ready for the Go-to function.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be selected or the interface does not recognize the AGV selection.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The visible step list showing 'Select an AGV' and the map-selection context.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the AGV cannot be selected from the map.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 1. Select an AGV 2. Select Go to 3. Select which square you want the AGV to go to 4. Select Confirm

steps.title: Select Go to
steps.purpose: Open the Go-to function for the selected AGV.
steps.expected_result: The Go-to function is activated for the selected AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The interface accepts the Go-to selection and is ready for destination selection.
steps.failure_condition: The Go-to control is unavailable or does not activate for the selected AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The visible step list showing 'Select Go to' in the Robot Selection Go-to function.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the Go-to function is not available for the selected AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 1. Select an AGV 2. Select Go to 3. Select which square you want the AGV to go to 4. Select Confirm

steps.title: Select the hospital destination square
steps.purpose: Choose the map square that will send the AGV to the hospital drop-off location in the recovery example.
steps.instruction: Select the destination square used to drop the AGV off at the hospital.
steps.expected_result: A destination square is selected for the AGV's Go-to movement toward the hospital.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended square is selected and corresponds to the hospital drop-off concept described in the source.
steps.failure_condition: No square is selected, the wrong square is selected, or the operator cannot determine the correct hospital destination square from the available source guidance.

actions.label: Choose destination square
actions.exact_action: Select which square you want the AGV to go to.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The visible step list showing square selection and the recovery text describing drop-off at the hospital.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the correct hospital destination square cannot be determined from the source and local map context.
Stop and escalate if the selected square does not represent the intended hospital drop-off area.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Select which square you want the AGV to go to.
Use the go to function to drop it off at the hospital.

steps.title: Confirm the Go-to command
steps.purpose: Submit the selected destination so the AGV can be repositioned.
steps.expected_result: The Go-to command is confirmed for the selected AGV and destination square.
steps.healthy_condition: The system accepts the confirmation and the AGV is committed to the selected destination.
steps.failure_condition: The confirmation is not accepted or the AGV does not begin the expected repositioning behavior.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The visible step list showing 'Select Confirm' as the final Go-to action.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop and escalate if the system does not accept the confirmation.
Stop and escalate if the AGV does not proceed as expected after confirmation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 1. Select an AGV 2. Select Go to 3. Select which square you want the AGV to go to 4. Select Confirm

steps.title: Remove the tote from the AGV
steps.purpose: Complete the source-described condition for the AGV to pick up a new task.
steps.instruction: Remove the tote from the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The tote is removed from the AGV and the AGV becomes able to pick up a new task as described by the source.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote has been removed and the AGV picks up a new task.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not removed or the AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.

actions.label: Remove tote
actions.exact_action: Remove the tote from the AGV.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGV does not resume by picking up a new task after tote removal.
Stop and escalate if tote removal cannot be completed safely or with approved site handling.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The AGV will pickup a new task once it’s tote has been removed.

success_criteria: The AGV is dropped off at the hospital using the Go-to function.
The tote is removed from the AGV.
The AGV picks up a new task after tote removal.
healthy_conditions: The affected AGV can be identified and selected on the map.
The Go-to function is available for the selected AGV.
A destination square can be selected and confirmed.
After tote removal, the AGV picks up a new task.
failure_conditions: The AGV cannot be identified or selected on the map.
The remove/add AGV action cannot be performed or is not permitted.
The correct hospital destination square cannot be determined.
The Go-to command cannot be confirmed.
The AGV does not pick up a new task after tote removal.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for SME or supervisor review before operational use because the source does not define the remove/add AGV subprocedure.
Escalate if permissions are insufficient to remove and add the AGV.
Escalate if the AGV does not resume by picking up a new task after tote removal, because no further recovery steps are provided in this source.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Training slide with the quick recovery example and the four Go-to steps.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: This is helpful when trying to recover quickly. Example if a Tipper goes down and an AGV will not leave... remove and add the agv and use the go to function to drop it off at the hospital.
The AGV will pickup a new task once it’s tote has been removed.

missing_details: The source does not define how to perform the remove and add AGV subprocedure.
The source does not specify the exact hospital destination square or map coordinates.
The source does not provide explicit validation checks showing how to confirm the AGV has reached the hospital.
The source does not provide timing expectations for task resumption after tote removal.
The source does not define role-based authorization boundaries beyond indirect evidence that some users may lack remove/add permissions.
